questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. A bolt from the blue
questions.explanation: A bolt from the blue – a sudden and unexpected event.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Jibe
questions.explanation: Jibe – make insulting or mocking remarksApprove – give consentAdmire – appreciate

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. I was surprise/ at his bad conduct/ as he was considered/ an obedient student.
questions.explanation: “I was surprised” in place of I was surprise. The sentence is in passive form of past indefinite sentence. Active --> Sub + v2 + object Passive --> sub + was/were + v3 + by + subject

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Hardly had he/ finished the task/ till the alarm rang.
questions.explanation: hardly is followed by when. So, till should be replaced with when.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. Fight shy of
questions.explanation: Fight shy of – to avoid a person or thing.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Solace
questions.explanation: Solace – Succour – to offer help to somebody in trouble.Agitate – to annoy

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. The player said, “The rules were broken in the match”.
options: The player said that the rules had been broken in the match.
The player said that the rules were broke in the match.
The player said that there was breaking of rules in the match.
The player said that the rules broke in the match.
questions.explanation: in Direct Past indefinite changes into past perfect in indirect form.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. a speech spoken to oneself, even if other people are around.
questions.explanation: Prologue – a piece of writing that introduces a play, poem, etc.Somniloquy – the act of talking in sleepMonologue – a long speech by a person

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Callous
questions.explanation: Callous - a stone hearted person who doesn’t care about feeling of othersCompassionate – kindApathetic – no caring about the feeling of othersIndifferent – not interested in or caring about something

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. He teaches us english.
options: English was taught to us by him.
English will be taught to us by him.
we are taught English by him.
we were teached English by him.
questions.explanation: Active --> Sub + v1 + Object Passive --> Object + is/am/are + v3 + by + sub

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. They stopped at a village on the way to rest and drink water and found the entire village had gathered to watch a weightlifter who was putting on a performance. B. Tenali was very impressed and exclaimed, “You are very strong!” C. With his big arms and bulging muscles, he picked up a 200-kg bag of rice with ease. D. One day Tenali Raman and his wife were on their way to Hampi.
questions.explanation: D is the introductory statement introducing the subject of the para i.e. the trip. It should be followed by A. Also, B should follow C because Tenali was impressed by the weightlifter.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. Suresh has only been working h̲e̲r̲e̲ ̲s̲i̲n̲c̲e̲ ̲o̲n̲e̲ ̲m̲o̲n̲t̲h̲ and he has already learnt the job.
questions.explanation: when a definite point of time is given, we use since. But, when a period of time is given, for is used.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who thinks that good things are more likely to happen or who emphasises the good part of a situation
questions.explanation: Misanthropist – a person who hates or avoids other people.Pessimist – a person who always look at the dark side of things.Complainer - someone who complains a lot about his problems

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Pompous
questions.explanation: Pompous – to consider oneself more important than others

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The new car starts to ………………….. in value as soon as it is driven off the lot.
questions.explanation: deteriorate - to make inferior in qualityDepreciate – to lessen in price or estimated value.Disparage – criticizeDenigrate – criticizeDepreciate is the most appropriate word.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: “professor” is the correct spelling.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Receipt is the correct spelling.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Shopkeeper: “Here, I have packed them all.” B. Mala: “Please give me a packet of balloons, two packets of streamers, and some candles.” C. Shopkeeper: “Since we are offering some discount on the balloons, you need to pay only thirty rupees.” D. Mala: “How much do I need to pay?”
questions.explanation: Considering the sequence of the conversation, a is the correct option

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. She had been reluctant to mention her idea before, but now she is …………… enough to risk his anger.
questions.explanation: Desperate – ready to anything to change the situation you are in.Critical – saying what is wrong with somebody/something

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. Walking along the road, a truck knocked him down.
options: While he was walking along the road
Since he was walking along the road
As he was walking along the road
questions.explanation: while he was must be added before walking otherwise it gives a wrong sense of truck as the subject.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Tourism is one (1)______ the biggest businesses in the world, generating (2)______ 200 million jobs. It is vital for the economies of (3)______ countries. The downside is that tourism is a major (4)______ of climate change through the carbon (5)______ it causes. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (1).
questions.explanation: one of the ….. of is the only fitting option here.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Tourism is one (1)______ the biggest businesses in the world, generating (2)______ 200 million jobs. It is vital for the economies of (3)______ countries. The downside is that tourism is a major (4)______ of climate change through the carbon (5)______ it causes. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (2).
questions.explanation: at least is the most appropriate option

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Tourism is one (1)______ the biggest businesses in the world, generating (2)______ 200 million jobs. It is vital for the economies of (3)______ countries.
